justification: Conflicting patterns identified with both described in detail. Pattern A count (3 services) and Pattern B count (2 services, more recent) both noted. Escalation to Coach K for pattern resolution is present and well-reasoned. However, no implementation was produced — Shaq blocked entirely without writing any code or producing output with a 'pending confirmation' notation. The rubric pass criteria require 'Implementation produced using one pattern with explicit pending confirmation notation' and 'deviations section documents the pattern choice as an assumption.' Neither exists. This hits the failure mode 'Blocking entirely on the escalation without producing any useful output.' The escalation itself is high quality and formally structured, which keeps this above fail, but the lack of any implementation output prevents a pass.

observations.text: Both patterns clearly identified with service counts, relative age, and structural differences
Escalation is formal, well-structured, and routed to the right people (Coach K → MJ/Bird)
Correctly noted Pattern B's recency as a competing signal against Pattern A's majority adoption
Correctly identified the structural incompatibility between constructor signatures and wiring
No implementation produced at all — rubric requires code with 'pending confirmation' notation
No deviations section documenting the pattern choice as an assumption
Hits explicit failure mode: 'Blocking entirely on the escalation without producing any useful output'
No acceptance_criteria_coverage noting which ACs are implemented vs dependent on resolution
